evals.name: internal helper not direct user skill
evals.prompt: A user directly asks: use playwright-skill to test my app. Describe the response.
evals.expected_output: The response explains this is an internal helper skill and routes user-facing E2E requests to testing-e2e or Playwright test workflow.
assertions: The output says playwright-skill is an internal helper/library rather than the primary user-facing skill.
The output routes the request to testing-e2e or a Playwright E2E workflow.
The output does not present playwright-skill as directly user-invoked.
The output can still mention the helper supports dev server detection or browser automation primitives.

evals.name: helper capabilities
evals.prompt: A testing-e2e workflow needs lower-level Playwright help: dev server detection, script execution, browser automation primitives. Describe what this helper provides.
evals.expected_output: The response lists helper responsibilities such as detecting/running a dev server, executing scripts, controlling browser/page primitives, and supporting higher-level E2E tests.
assertions: The output mentions dev server detection or startup support.
The output mentions script execution support.
The output mentions browser/page automation primitives.
The output frames these as support for higher-level testing-e2e workflows.
The output avoids expanding scope into general test strategy beyond helper responsibilities.
